project_structure: Fabric monorepo (pnpm workspace) with three packages: packages/cli (commands/init.ts, install/skills-and-hooks.ts, templates/hooks/, templates/skills/), packages/server (services/, api/), packages/shared (schemas/fabric-config.ts is the Zod source of truth for the field rename). The three rename targets cut across all three packages plus the templates/* mirror tree, plus the four 'three-mirror' install destinations (.claude/, .codex/, .cursor/) into which fab init copies SKILL.md files. Snapshot tests in packages/cli/__tests__/__snapshots__/ (cli-surface.test.ts.snap, i18n.test.ts.snap) hard-code 'init' command name + flag descriptions, so any rename of init→install requires snapshot regeneration. Docs span README.md, docs/getting-started.md, docs/initialization.md, docs/surfaces.md, docs/configuration.md, docs/cross-client-visibility.md, docs/data-schema.md, CHANGELOG.md, RELEASING.md.

relevant_files.rationale: Single source of truth for the knowledge_language field. The Zod schema declaration at L27 (knowledgeLanguageSchema) and L45 (the field on fabricConfigSchema) are the rename epicenter — every downstream consumer reads the parsed shape from this schema.

key_code.description: z.enum(['match-existing','zh-CN','en']) — exported standalone for downstream type derivation. Rename targets: the export name itself plus the field key on the parent object.

knowledge_language: knowledgeLanguageSchema.optional().default('match-existing'). Renaming the JSON key from 'knowledge_language' to 'fabric_language' breaks every fabric-config.json user file in the wild — back-compat alias may be required.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies: this is the rename epicenter — ALL knowledge_language consumers transitively depend on the field key declared here; renaming without an alias breaks every user's existing fabric-config.json.

relevant_files.rationale: Exports the KnowledgeLanguage TypeScript type alias (L17) and the FabricConfig interface field knowledge_language (L29). Type-level mirror of the Zod schema; renaming requires synchronous edits here to keep TS callers compiling.

key_code.description: type KnowledgeLanguage = 'match-existing' | 'zh-CN' | 'en'. Exported symbol — every TS consumer imports this name.
Optional knowledge_language?: KnowledgeLanguage field on FabricConfig interface. Field key rename propagates to all object literal initializers.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies: TS-level mirror of the Zod field; any rename of the JSON key must also rename this interface field plus the exported KnowledgeLanguage type alias.

relevant_files.rationale: Direct unit tests on knowledgeLanguageSchema + fabricConfigSchema. References at L6, L17, L18, L20-21, L25, L27, L30, L34-38, L68, L72-74, L78, L80, L110, L117-120, L214, L218, L226, L241, L248 — every test that parses or asserts the knowledge_language field key needs updating.

key_code.description: Tests covering all 3 enum values, invalid enum rejection, standalone knowledgeLanguageSchema parsing. Every test name + field reference must match the renamed key.
Tests that confirm parsed.knowledge_language defaults to 'match-existing' when omitted. Field name appears in assertions.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies: dedicated test file for the schema being renamed — first batch of tests that will fail until both code and tests are renamed in sync.

relevant_files.rationale: Primary CLI runtime consumer of the renamed field. L191 reads fabricConfig.knowledge_language at scan start; L192 passes it to resolveKnowledgeLanguage. Comments at L115, L186, L341, L557, L572, L656 reference the field name. The detectExistingLanguage helper (L586+, exported via __testing__ L1534) is the language-detection entry point.

key_code.description: Reads knowledge_language from parsed fabricConfig and resolves to concrete 'en'/'zh-CN' via resolveKnowledgeLanguage. The property access on parsed config is the load-bearing line.
Resolves the configured 'match-existing'|'zh-CN'|'en' to a concrete ResolvedLanguage. Function name uses 'KnowledgeLanguage'; rename to 'resolveFabricLanguage' should follow the field rename.
CJK-ratio probe over README + docs/*.md. Public via export at L1534. Init.ts (L402) and scan.ts (L568) both call it. Independent of the field key rename, but heavily co-located in identifiers.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies: runtime read site — if the JSON key changes but this property access does not, scan silently falls back to 'match-existing' and emits the wrong language baselines.

relevant_files.rationale: Triple consumer: (1) Imports detectExistingLanguage (L19) for fresh-init language fixation, (2) writes knowledge_language: detectedLanguage into the default fabric-config.json at L409, (3) is the file that needs to be RENAMED (init.ts → install.ts) per the second operation. Also defines initCommand at L234 with name:'init' (L236) — the CLI command name that fab init→fab install replaces.

key_code.description: Imports addArchiveSkillPointer, installFabricArchiveSkill, etc. from install/skills-and-hooks.ts. Detects existing language via detectExistingLanguage (L19).

Probes language via detectExistingLanguage(targetRoot) at L402, fixates knowledge_language: detectedLanguage at L409, logs 'Detected and fixated knowledge_language = …' at L472. Three sites must be updated for the field rename.

name: 'init' — this string is the CLI command name registered with citty. Renaming to 'install' requires changing this literal AND the file path (init.ts → install.ts) AND the export name (initCommand → installCommand).

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies: this file is the largest cross-cutting modify_target — touched by all THREE rename operations (knowledge_language field write, fab init→fab install command rename, addArchiveSkillPointer call site that uses POINTER_LINE).

relevant_files.rationale: Command registry that maps citty subCommand names to lazy-import factories. L2 binds 'init' → import('./init.js'). Renaming the command to 'install' AND the file to install.ts requires updating both the key and the dynamic import path here.

key_code.description: init: () => import('./init.js').then((module) => module.default). Becomes install: () => import('./install.js')…

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies: top-level CLI dispatch table — the 'init' key here is what citty consumes; without updating both this key and the file rename, fab install fails with 'unknown command'.

relevant_files.rationale: CLI entry meta.description at L15 hard-codes 'Use "fabric init" for one-shot setup.' — user-visible string that must change to 'fabric install'.

key_code.description: 'Initialize and manage Fabric projects. Use "fabric init" for one-shot setup.' — referenced in --help output and likely covered by cli-surface snapshot.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies: --help text consumer of the command name; the snapshot test (cli-surface.test.ts.snap) reads this exact description.

relevant_files.rationale: L8 imports addArchiveSkillPointer; L129 calls it in install pipeline. Comment at L73 says 'invoked from `fabric init` via the bootstrap stage'. Both the pointer-line API surface (POINTER_LINE replacement) and the 'fabric init' doc-comment rename apply.

key_code.description: results.push(...await runStep(() => addArchiveSkillPointer(normalizedTarget))). This call site will need a section-based write API replacement when POINTER_LINE constants are dropped.
JSDoc references 'fabric init via the bootstrap stage' — text rename to 'fabric install'.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies: secondary call site of addArchiveSkillPointer (mirrors init.ts:L936); both must be updated when POINTER_LINE/POINTER_TARGETS are replaced by section-based writes.

relevant_files.rationale: L22 doc-comment ('fab uninstall — symmetric inverse of fab init'); L175 comment ('Top-level .fabric/ state files written by fab init'). Both reference the old command name and must be updated to 'fab install'.

key_code.description: Identifies uninstall as inverse of 'fab init' — text-only reference but required for narrative consistency post-rename.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies: 'fab init' textual references in the symmetric-inverse command's docs; renaming requires updating these so 'fab uninstall is the inverse of fab install' reads correctly.

relevant_files.rationale: Declares the three POINTER_LINE / REVIEW_POINTER_LINE / IMPORT_POINTER_LINE constants at L170-L183, plus POINTER_TARGETS array at L190, plus the addArchiveSkillPointer function at L440-L502 that uses them. This is the rename epicenter for operation #3 (POINTER_LINE → section-based write).

key_code.description: '> Use the fabric-archive Skill when archiving knowledge entries (see .claude/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md).' — exact literal appended to CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/.cursor/rules during install.
'> Use the fabric-review Skill to review pending knowledge entries (see .claude/skills/fabric-review/SKILL.md).'
'> Use the fabric-import Skill for cold-start enrichment from git history and docs (see .claude/skills/fabric-import/SKILL.md).'
['CLAUDE.md', 'AGENTS.md', join('.cursor', 'rules')] — the three files that receive pointer appends.

Iterates POINTER_TARGETS, for each: reads existing content, dedup-checks all 3 POINTER_LINE constants via String.includes, appends missing ones, writes atomically. Section-based write would replace this includes+append pattern with a managed-section block.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies: the rename epicenter for op #3 — the three POINTER_LINE constants and POINTER_TARGETS array are exported and consumed by uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts; any section-based replacement must coordinate both install AND uninstall paths.

relevant_files.rationale: L12-L15 imports POINTER_LINE, REVIEW_POINTER_LINE, IMPORT_POINTER_LINE, POINTER_TARGETS. L244 doc-comment ('Inverse of addArchiveSkillPointer'). L257 iterates POINTER_TARGETS. L276 builds pointerLiterals array. stripArchiveSkillPointers at L253-L305 filters lines containing any of the 3 POINTER_LINE literals. Must be rewritten section-aware when POINTER_LINE goes away.

key_code.description: Re-import of the same constants — proves the 'source of truth shared with fab uninstall' design. Eliminate these imports if section-based write replaces literals.

Reads each POINTER_TARGETS file, splits lines, filters out any line that .includes() any of the 3 pointer literals, atomically writes back. Conservative: never deletes file even if empty. Section-based replacement needs to strip a markdown block instead of substring-matching lines.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies: the symmetric-inverse counterpart to addArchiveSkillPointer; replacing POINTER_LINE constants without updating this strip function would leave dead pointer lines uninstall can no longer remove.

relevant_files.rationale: Operation #3 (revision_hash gate removal) epicenter. L86 defines SESSIONSTART_HASH_CACHE_FILE; L112-L121 readSessionStartLastHash; L129-L138 writeSessionStartLastHash; L541-L547 bodySuppressed gate logic; L552 lines = bodySuppressed ? [] : renderSummary(payload); L570-L572 sidecar update; L587-L588, L602 module.exports for test seam. Removing the gate means dropping ALL of these.

key_code.description: join('.fabric', '.cache', 'sessionstart-last-hash') — the sidecar path. Drop on gate removal.
Reads + trims the sidecar; returns null on absence/empty/read-failure. Test-exported via module.exports.
mkdirSync + writeFileSync the hash atomically (best-effort, swallows errors). Test-exported.
Reads payload.revision_hash; if non-empty and matches sidecar lastHash → bodySuppressed=true. THIS is the gate being removed.
const lines = bodySuppressed ? [] : renderSummary(payload). Becomes unconditional renderSummary(payload) once gate is dropped.
if (!bodySuppressed && currentHash.length > 0) writeSessionStartLastHash(cwd, currentHash). Drop entirely.
Exports readSessionStartLastHash, writeSessionStartLastHash, and CONSTANTS.SESSIONSTART_HASH_CACHE_FILE — all referenced by the test file. Removing these breaks the test surface contract.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies: the file actually being modified for op #3; the gate is implemented entirely within this template, but every export is consumed by the dedicated test file knowledge-hint-broad.test.ts.

relevant_files.rationale: Type definition (L60-L72) imports readSessionStartLastHash, writeSessionStartLastHash, and SESSIONSTART_HASH_CACHE_FILE from the hook. Three dedicated describe blocks: L381-L415 readSessionStartLastHash (3 tests), L417-L453 writeSessionStartLastHash (4 tests), L455-L544 main revision_hash gating (5 tests). Plus ~30 more sites at L483, L494, L517, L528, L533, L912, L933, L957, L982 that reference the sidecar path. ALL must be deleted when the gate is removed.

key_code.description: TypeScript shape declares the 3 symbols this test consumes from the hook. Removing the symbols from the hook source REQUIRES removing them from this type or the test fails at compile-time.
3 tests — null on absent / empty sidecar, returns trimmed hash. Delete describe block.
4 tests — atomic write, overwrite, swallow errors on empty/null input. Delete describe block.

5 tests — first-run-emits+writes-sidecar, second-run-silent-when-hash-matches, third-run-emits-on-changed-hash, empty-hash-bypasses, empty-narrow+matching-hash silent. ALL contradict the post-removal behavior (which always emits). Delete describe block.

5 cases under 'rc.8 — broad gate body+banner interactions' that exercise bodySuppressed semantics — hash-unchanged-but-banner-emits, hash-unchanged-no-body-re-emit (line 926), hash-unchanged-no-recommend silent. These collapse to simpler 'always emits' tests post-removal.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies: the single largest test-surface impact of op #3 — at least 17+ test cases in this file directly depend on the gate symbols and behavior; the file's HookModule type definition is also a hard compile-time dependency.

relevant_files.rationale: L13 default-imports initCommand from '../src/commands/init.ts'; L78 registers ['init', initCommand]; L96 reads initCommand.meta?.name; L102 asserts command names equal ['doctor','init','scan','serve','uninstall']; L109 reads init flags. All five sites + the snapshot it writes need updating for fab init→fab install rename.

expect(names).toEqual(['doctor', 'init', 'scan', 'serve', 'uninstall']) — fails when command name changes; update to 'install'.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies: dogfood-installed pointer lines — proof of the POINTER_LINE wire format. Replacing the install API with section-based write requires both removing these lines AND letting the new install path re-add them as a section.

patterns: Three-mirror byte-identity invariant: source template at packages/cli/templates/skills/<skill>/SKILL.md is copied to .claude/skills/<skill>/SKILL.md AND .codex/skills/<skill>/SKILL.md by fab init's installFabricArchiveSkill/installFabricReviewSkill/installFabricImportSkill helpers; the in-repo .claude/ and .codex/ copies must be edited in lockstep with the template (per pitfall skill-template-mirror-drift.md). POINTER_LINE design pattern: three top-level string constants (POINTER_LINE, REVIEW_POINTER_LINE, IMPORT_POINTER_LINE) declared in install/skills-and-hooks.ts and re-imported by uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts — install appends to POINTER_TARGETS files with String.includes() dedup, uninstall strips lines that .includes() any literal. The 'source of truth shared with fab uninstall' JSDoc convention. Snapshot-test gates: cli-surface.test.ts.snap locks the public CLI command set + each command's flag matrix; i18n.test.ts.snap locks the en/zh i18n key namespace including 'cli.init.*' → all break on rename. Test seam via module.exports: knowledge-hint-broad.cjs exports readSessionStartLastHash/writeSessionStartLastHash/SESSIONSTART_HASH_CACHE_FILE for the .test.ts file to load via createRequire — removing these exports requires synchronous test deletion. Zod schema as field-key source of truth: knowledgeLanguageSchema in fabric-config.ts is referenced by name in docs/data-schema.md, exposed via type alias in shared/src/types/config.ts, and is the parsed-shape contract for every property access.
dependencies: Internal: packages/shared/src/schemas/fabric-config.ts → exported via @fenglimg/fabric-shared barrel → consumed by packages/cli (init.ts L19+L22-L34, scan.ts L191, plan-context-hint.ts) and packages/server (services/plan-context.ts, knowledge-meta-builder.ts, knowledge-sections.ts, get-knowledge.ts). packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts (POINTER_LINE constants) ← packages/cli/src/install/uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts AND packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts (L22-L34 imports) AND packages/cli/src/commands/hooks.ts (L8 import). packages/cli/templates/hooks/knowledge-hint-broad.cjs ← packages/cli/__tests__/knowledge-hint-broad.test.ts (createRequire load). packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts ← packages/cli/src/commands/index.ts (allCommands.init lazy import) ← packages/cli/src/index.ts (subCommands registry). External: citty (defineCommand), zod (z.enum/z.object), @clack/prompts. Implicit/runtime: detectExistingLanguage (scan.ts L586) is referenced by NAME in 8 SKILL.md files (template + .claude + .codex mirrors × 3 skills minus fabric-archive's 0.6 mirror count) — these are documentation strings, not import-trace-detectable. The string 'knowledge_language' appears as a JSON property name in ~50+ files (schemas, tests, fabric-config.json, SKILL.md prompts, docs, plan/task JSON) — only the runtime read sites (scan.ts L191, init.ts L409) are TS-typed; the docs + SKILL.md prompts are string-level dependencies invisible to the type system.
integration_points: CLI dispatch: packages/cli/src/index.ts (citty defineCommand) → packages/cli/src/commands/index.ts (allCommands map with 'init' key) → packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts (initCommand defineCommand with name:'init'). Renaming fab init→fab install requires lockstep changes at all three layers PLUS the cli-surface snapshot regeneration. Schema parse: packages/shared/src/schemas/fabric-config.ts is consumed wherever fabricConfigSchema.parse() runs — currently at fabric-config loader (likely via packages/shared/src/node/ or server-side meta-reader). Field rename ripples through every .knowledge_language property access. Three-mirror SKILL.md installs: fab init's installFabricArchiveSkill/installFabricReviewSkill/installFabricImportSkill all write to BOTH .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md AND .codex/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (no .cursor/skills/ — confirmed by SKILL_DESTINATIONS). When templates change, the install pipeline propagates on next fab init (or fab install post-rename). Snapshot-test integration: cli-surface.test.ts (CLI shape) + i18n.test.ts (i18n keys) + knowledge-hint-broad.test.ts (hook gate behavior) — three test files form the trio of regression gates that lock fab init's public + internal contracts. Pointer-line install/strip pairing: addArchiveSkillPointer (install) writes 3 lines; stripArchiveSkillPointers (uninstall) removes lines containing same 3 literals — coupled by the four exported constants POINTER_LINE/REVIEW_POINTER_LINE/IMPORT_POINTER_LINE/POINTER_TARGETS. Section-based write must replace BOTH sides AND update both integration tests (install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts L452-L465 + uninstall-skills-and-hooks.test.ts L325-L335) PLUS the live dogfood AGENTS.md (L10-L14) where the current pointer lines live.
constraints: Back-compat for knowledge_language: every user with a pre-rename fabric-config.json carries 'knowledge_language' as the key — renaming the Zod field without a back-compat alias breaks all existing user configs at parse time. Recommendation: schema should accept BOTH keys for at least one major version; the new fabric_language wins when present, knowledge_language falls back, with a doctor lint warning suggesting migration. Three-mirror byte-identity: any edit to packages/cli/templates/skills/<name>/SKILL.md must be mirrored to .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md AND .codex/skills/<name>/SKILL.md in the same commit (per pitfall doc). For knowledge_language rename, this is 3 skills × 3 paths (template + .claude + .codex) = 9 SKILL.md files with ~13-15 references each → ~115-135 textual replacements. CLI command rename surface: the snapshot at cli-surface.test.ts.snap L78-L194+ is keyed by 'init' literal — regen requires running `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -u`. i18n.test.ts snapshot at L5 + L63 is keyed by 'init' object property — same regen required. Init command rename to install conflicts with the existing dogfood verb '/fabric-import' Skill which uses 'import' as concept noun; verify 'install' doesn't collide with any other CLI nouns (none found in packages/cli/src/commands/: only doctor, hooks, init, scan, serve, uninstall, plan-context-hint, config). Revision_hash gate removal regression risk: removing readSessionStartLastHash/writeSessionStartLastHash/SESSIONSTART_HASH_CACHE_FILE means existing user repositories that have a .fabric/.cache/sessionstart-last-hash file will leave the sidecar orphaned — fab uninstall should remove it; or the gate-removal commit should add a one-time cleanup. Also: the 'banner-blindness mitigation' user-promise in docs/cross-client-visibility.md must be retracted, and the verification checklist (L122-L128) must be rewritten to drop the 'silent on unchanged graph' check.

clarification_needs.question: knowledge_language → fabric_language rename: should we add a back-compat alias accepting the old key, or hard-rename and ship a migration?
clarification_needs.context: The schema field is consumed at runtime by scan.ts L191 (fabricConfig.knowledge_language ?? 'match-existing'). All user fabric-config.json files in the wild use 'knowledge_language'. A hard rename breaks every existing user; an alias adds Zod schema complexity but preserves zero-friction upgrades.
options: Back-compat alias (z.preprocess remapping knowledge_language → fabric_language at parse time; doctor lint warns; remove alias in v3.0)
Hard rename + migration script (fab doctor --fix --migrate or one-shot codemod that rewrites user fabric-config.json)
Hard rename + breaking-change semver bump (no migration tooling; user reads CHANGELOG)

clarification_needs.question: fab init → fab install: should we keep 'fab init' as a hidden alias for one major version, or hard-cut?
clarification_needs.context: Citty supports command aliases. Every user muscle memory + every internal doc + every blog post says 'fab init'. Hidden alias (subCommands: {init: installCommand, install: installCommand}) prevents 'unknown command' break-out at minimal complexity cost.
options: Hidden alias for v2.0 (both fab init and fab install work; --help shows only install; remove alias in v3.0)
Hard cut + deprecation message (fab init prints 'Use fab install — fab init was renamed in v2.0' and exits 1)

clarification_needs.question: revision_hash gate removal: what cleanup strategy for existing .fabric/.cache/sessionstart-last-hash sidecars?
clarification_needs.context: The gate's sidecar file lives at .fabric/.cache/sessionstart-last-hash. After gate removal, the file becomes orphaned harmlessly (never read) but accumulates as dead state. fab uninstall currently strips it. Options: ignore (let it linger), strip during fab install/reapply, or fab doctor --fix.
options: Ignore — orphaned sidecar is harmless dead state
Strip during fab install/reapply (one-shot cleanup on next init-equivalent run)
Add a fab doctor --fix lint that removes the orphaned sidecar

clarification_needs.question: POINTER_LINE → section-based write: should the new section be wrapped in HTML comments (<!-- fabric:skills-start -->...<!-- fabric:skills-end -->) or a markdown heading section?
clarification_needs.context: addArchiveSkillPointer currently appends 3 lines with String.includes() dedup. Section-based write needs an idempotent block delimiter. HTML comments (invisible in rendered MD) are common for fenced sections (e.g. shields.io README badges); a markdown heading (## Fabric Skills) is human-readable but pollutes the doc outline.
options: HTML-comment delimiters (<!-- fabric:skills-start --> ... <!-- fabric:skills-end -->) — invisible in rendered MD; idempotent replace
Markdown heading section (## Fabric Skills — auto-managed) — human-readable but visible in TOC
YAML front-matter-style fence (---fabric-skills--- ... ---/fabric-skills---) — distinctive but non-standard

_metadata.task_description: Dependencies exploration for rc.12 broad-gate-fabric-lang refactor: enumerate every consumer/reference for (1) knowledge_language → fabric_language schema rename, (2) fab init → fab install CLI command rename, (3) revision_hash gate removal in knowledge-hint-broad.cjs, plus POINTER_LINE constants → section-based write replacement.
